title: carex remota subsp. stewartii
intro: Carex remota subsp. stewartii, also known as Stewart's sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Carex remota subsp. stewartii is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries.
